steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Infinitely many solutions means the two equations describe the same line, so the second equation must be a scalar multiple of the first.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Simplify the given equation by dividing by 5: $-15x+25y=65 \Rightarrow -3x+5y=13$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Test each choice: divide by 4 to simplify. A: $3x+5y=13$ (wrong sign on $x$). B: $3x+5y=-13$ (wrong signs). C: $-3x+5y=13$ (matches). D: $-3x+5y=-13$ (wrong constant sign).

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• '$12x+20y=52$': Divides to $3x+5y=13$, but the $x$-coefficient should be negative. ✗
  • '$12x+20y=-52$': Divides to $3x+5y=-13$, wrong signs on both $x$ and constant. ✗
  • '$-12x+20y=52$': Divides to $-3x+5y=13$, which is exactly the simplified given equation. ✓
  • '$-12x+20y=-52$': Divides to $-3x+5y=-13$, wrong sign on constant. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
